name: Pawn1st
description_short: Located at 7075 E 22nd St, Tucson, AZ 85710, Pawn1st can be reached at +1 520-748-2274.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn store operator with over 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and Latin America (Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador). The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is listed in both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index, indicating its scale and public market standing.

FirstCash's core business model focuses on serving cash and credit-constrained consumers through retail pawn operations. They buy and sell a wide variety of merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. The company's primary lending product is non-recourse pawn loans, where customers pledge personal property as collateral in exchange for immediate cash. Beyond traditional pawn loans, FirstCash offers layaway services (10% down payment plans), gold and precious metal buying, and retail merchandise sales. Through its wholly owned subsidiary AFF, the company also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale—operating the most pawn store locations of any competitor—and geographic diversity across multiple countries and regions. The company's dual focus on pawn lending and technology-driven payment solutions through AFF sets it apart from single-service competitors. Their established presence in both developed markets (UK) and developing markets (Latin America) demonstrates operational complexity and brand recognition.

As a pawn lender, FirstCash serves consumers who need immediate cash without credit checks or lengthy approval processes. However, customers should understand that pawn loans are short-term solutions with risk of losing pledged items if loans aren't repaid. The non-recourse nature of their loans protects borrowers from debt collection, but they lose their collateral entirely. Interest rates and fees on pawn loans are typically higher than traditional personal loans, making this best suited for emergency situations rather than long-term financing needs.
pros: Largest pawn store network with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, UK, and Latin America for convenient access
Immediate cash available on the spot with no credit check or approval delays required
Non-recourse loans protect borrowers from debt collection if unable to repay
Wide variety of merchandise accepted including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, and musical instruments
Gold and precious metal buying service for quick cash conversion of valuables
Layaway service with affordable 10% down payment option for retail purchases
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) providing financial transparency and stability
cons: Loss of pledged collateral if loan cannot be repaid—no opportunity to reclaim items after default
Pawn loan rates and fees typically higher than traditional personal loans or credit alternatives
Limited loan amounts tied to assessed value of collateral, may not meet larger cash needs
Short repayment terms typical of pawn loans create pressure for rapid repayment
No credit-building opportunity—pawn loans do not report to credit bureaus
best_for: Consumers facing immediate cash emergencies with valuable personal property to pledge
Individuals with poor or no credit history unable to qualify for traditional loans
People seeking quick liquidity without lengthy application or approval processes
Those looking to sell gold, jewelry, or other valuables for immediate cash
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for consumers facing genuine cash emergencies who have valuable personal property they're willing to pledge as collateral and don't qualify for traditional credit products. The critical caveat is that pawn loans result in permanent loss of collateral if not repaid within the loan term—they should only be used for short-term liquidity needs, not ongoing credit solutions.
services: Pawn loans secured by pledged personal property
Gold and precious metal buying (immediate cash payment)
Retail merchandise sales (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Layaway plans with 10% down payment option
Lease-to-own payment solutions through AFF subsidiary
Retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partners
Store locator and inventory browsing tools
Non-recourse lending structure without debt collection risk
